# Stack Standards

> **This file is auto-generated by `/detect-stack`.** Do not edit manually — run `/detect-stack` to regenerate after adding or changing technologies in the project.

## Detected Stack

- **Node.js 24.x** (via engines in package.json) — LTS release
- **npm 10+** (via engines in package.json)
- **TypeScript 6.0.2** (beta) — cutting-edge pre-release
- **React 19.2.3** — latest stable with Actions, `use` hook, ref as prop
- **Vite 7.3.2** — latest build tool with SWC plugin
- **Tailwind CSS 4.1.18** — latest with new Vite plugin architecture
- **Vitest 4.0.16** — test runner with V8 coverage
- **ESLint 9.39.4** — flat config format
- **typescript-eslint 8.54.0** — latest TypeScript linting
- **Cloudflare Workers** — deployment target (via wrangler.jsonc)
- **@tanstack/react-query 5.90.20** — server state management
- **React Hook Form 7.71.1 + Zod 4.3.5** — form validation
- **Radix UI + shadcn/ui** — headless components + styled components
- **date-fns 4.1.0** — date manipulation
- **@testing-library/react 16.3.2** — React testing utilities
- **@github/spark** — GitHub's component framework
- **Framer Motion 12.38.0** — animation library
- **D3.js 7.9.0** — data visualization
- **Three.js 0.183.2** — 3D graphics

---

## TypeScript 6.0 (Beta)

<!-- Source: TypeScript 6.0 beta docs, TypeScript Handbook -->

✅ Enable `strict: true` and all strict checks — project already has `strictNullChecks`
✅ Use `type` imports via `import type { ... }` — enforced by ESLint `@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-imports`
✅ Use `unknown` instead of `any` — narrow with type guards or type predicates
✅ Use `interface` for object shapes that may be extended; `type` for unions, intersections, and mapped types
✅ Use `satisfies` operator for type-safe object literals while preserving literal types
✅ Use `as const` for readonly literal types and tuple types
✅ Prefer `readonly` modifier for props, array parameters, and immutable data
⛔ NEVER use `any` without explicit justification and tracking — prefer `unknown` and narrow
⛔ NEVER use `@ts-ignore` — use `@ts-expect-error` with explanation if suppression is unavoidable
⛔ NEVER use `require()` — use ES module `import` syntax exclusively
⛔ NEVER use `namespace` — use ES modules instead
⛔ NEVER disable `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` when enabled — handle potential `undefined` from index access

---

## React 19

<!-- Source: React 19 release docs, Rules of Hooks, React docs -->

✅ Use Actions for async state transitions — `useActionState`, `useOptimistic`, `useTransition`
✅ Use `<form action={asyncFunction}>` for form submissions — automatic pending states, error handling, and reset
✅ Use `useFormStatus` in child components to access parent form state
✅ Use `useOptimistic` for immediate UI feedback during async operations
✅ Use `use(promise)` to unwrap promises in render — only for promises from suspense-compatible libraries
✅ Use `use(context)` after early returns — unlike `useContext`, `use` can be conditional
✅ Accept `ref` as a normal prop in function components — `forwardRef` is legacy
✅ Render `<Context>` directly instead of `<Context.Provider>` — `Context.Provider` is legacy
✅ Return cleanup functions from ref callbacks — called on unmount
✅ Use `<title>`, `<meta>`, `<link>` directly in components — React hoists to `<head>` automatically
✅ Use `<link rel="stylesheet" href="..." precedence="default">` for stylesheets — React manages loading order
✅ Use `<script async src="...">` for async scripts — React deduplicates across components
✅ All hooks must follow Rules of Hooks — only call at top level, only call from React functions
✅ Custom hooks must start with `use` prefix
✅ Use Error Boundaries for error handling — errors in render should be caught
⛔ NEVER create promises inside render for `use()` — will trigger warnings; use cached promises from libraries
⛔ NEVER use `forwardRef` — use `ref` as a prop directly
⛔ NEVER use `<Context.Provider>` — use `<Context>` directly
⛔ NEVER call hooks conditionally or in loops — violates Rules of Hooks
⛔ NEVER call hooks after early returns or inside callbacks
⛔ NEVER mutate props or state directly — treat as immutable
⛔ NEVER use indexes as keys when order/identity changes — causes reconciliation bugs
⛔ NEVER suppress React Hook exhaustive-deps warnings without careful review — usually indicates missing dependencies

---

## Vite 7

<!-- Source: Vite 7 docs, Vite guide -->

✅ Use `@vitejs/plugin-react-swc` for fast refresh — already configured
✅ Use path aliases via `resolve.alias` in `vite.config.ts` — project uses `@/` for `src/`
✅ Use `import.meta.env` for environment variables — not `process.env` in client code
✅ Define public environment variables with `VITE_` prefix — others are server-only
✅ Place static assets in `public/` — referenced without `/public` prefix
✅ Use `?url` suffix to import asset URLs — `import logo from './logo.svg?url'`
✅ Use `?raw` suffix to import file contents as string
✅ Use `vite.config.ts` `test` field for Vitest configuration — unified config
✅ Use top-level `await` — Vite handles it correctly
⛔ NEVER use `require.context` — use `import.meta.glob` instead
⛔ NEVER access `process.env` in client code — use `import.meta.env`
⛔ NEVER import from `dist/` or build output — import from source
⛔ NEVER use `NODE_ENV` checks in client code without Vite replacing it

---

## Tailwind CSS 4

<!-- Source: Tailwind CSS v4 docs, Tailwind best practices -->

✅ Use `@tailwindcss/vite` plugin — project already configured
✅ Import Tailwind via `@import "tailwindcss"` in CSS — v4 syntax
✅ Use CSS variables for theme customization — `var(--color-accent-1)` pattern already in use
✅ Use `@theme` directive in CSS for custom design tokens
✅ Use arbitrary values for one-off styles — `w-[137px]`, `bg-[#1da1f2]`
✅ Use `@apply` sparingly — only for extracting repeated utility combinations, not for semantic class names
✅ Use `clsx` or `tailwind-merge` for conditional classes — project uses both
✅ Use `@container` queries via `@container` utility — requires explicit container
✅ Prefer composition of utility classes over custom CSS
⛔ NEVER use `@apply` for every style — defeats Tailwind's purpose
⛔ NEVER create semantic class names that wrap single utilities — use utilities directly
⛔ NEVER use `!important` to force specificity — use `!` prefix on utility instead: `!mt-4`
⛔ NEVER hardcode colors, spacing, or other theme values — use Tailwind tokens

---

## Vitest 4

<!-- Source: Vitest docs, project's vitest config -->

✅ Use `describe`, `it`/`test`, `expect` — Vitest globals enabled in config
✅ Use `vi.mock()` for module mocking — use `vi` not `jest`
✅ Use `vi.fn()` for function mocks
✅ Use `vi.spyOn()` for spying on object methods
✅ Use `beforeEach`/`afterEach` for test isolation
✅ Use `@testing-library/react` for component testing — already configured
✅ Use `screen` queries from Testing Library — `screen.getByRole`, `screen.getByText`
✅ Use `userEvent` from `@testing-library/user-event` for interactions — not `fireEvent`
✅ Write tests in `*.test.ts` or `*.spec.ts` files — convention in project
✅ Use `@testing-library/jest-dom` matchers — `toBeInTheDocument`, `toHaveTextContent`
✅ Coverage thresholds enforced at 70% for statements, branches, functions, lines
✅ Test environment is `jsdom` — browser APIs available
⛔ NEVER use `jest.fn()` or `jest.mock()` — use `vi.fn()`, `vi.mock()`
⛔ NEVER test implementation details — test user-visible behavior
⛔ NEVER use `wrapper.find()` or enzyme patterns — use Testing Library queries
⛔ NEVER use `getByTestId` as first choice — prefer accessibility queries (`getByRole`, `getByLabelText`)
⛔ NEVER suppress coverage for app code without clear justification

---

## ESLint 9 (Flat Config)

<!-- Source: ESLint 9 docs, typescript-eslint docs, project's eslint.config.js -->

✅ Use flat config format — `eslint.config.js` exporting array of config objects
✅ Use `files` globs to scope rules to specific file patterns
✅ Use `ignores` array for exclusions — project ignores `dist/`, `coverage/`, `.wrangler/`
✅ Use `languageOptions.globals` to define global variables
✅ Import plugins directly — `import tseslint from 'typescript-eslint'`
✅ Use `typescript-eslint` recommended config — `...tseslint.configs.recommended`
✅ Use `@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-imports` with `separate-type-imports` — enforced in project
✅ Use underscore prefix for unused variables — `_unusedVar` pattern allowed
✅ Disable `react-refresh/only-export-components` for `components/ui/**` — generated shadcn/ui code
⛔ NEVER use legacy `.eslintrc.json` format
⛔ NEVER ignore ESLint errors without fixing or documented justification
⛔ NEVER disable `react-hooks/rules-of-hooks` or `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps`
⛔ NEVER use `eslint-disable` comments without explanation

---

## Cloudflare Workers

<!-- Source: Cloudflare Workers docs, Wrangler docs -->

✅ Keep bundles small — Workers have size limits (1MB compressed for free tier)
✅ Use standard `Request`/`Response` Web APIs — Workers runtime, not Node.js
✅ Use `fetch` for HTTP requests — native to Workers runtime
✅ Avoid Node.js built-ins unless polyfilled — no `fs`, `path`, `crypto` (use Web Crypto API)
✅ Use `wrangler.jsonc` for Workers configuration
✅ Use `compatibility_date` to pin Workers runtime behavior
✅ Cold starts matter — optimize initialization code
✅ Use `env` bindings for secrets and environment variables
✅ Use KV, Durable Objects, or R2 for persistent storage — Workers have no filesystem
⛔ NEVER use Node.js APIs directly — Workers runtime is not Node.js
⛔ NEVER use `process.env` in Workers — use `env` bindings passed to fetch handler
⛔ NEVER assume filesystem access — use KV or R2 for storage
⛔ NEVER use long-running operations — Workers have CPU time limits
⛔ NEVER bundle unnecessary dependencies — increases cold start time

---

## TanStack Query (React Query) 5

<!-- Source: TanStack Query v5 docs -->

✅ Use `QueryClient` with `QueryClientProvider` at app root
✅ Use `useQuery` for GET/read operations — automatic caching, background refetch
✅ Use `useMutation` for POST/PUT/DELETE operations — manual triggering
✅ Use `queryKey` arrays for cache identification — `['todos', { status: 'done' }]`
✅ Use `queryFn` to define data fetching logic
✅ Use `staleTime` to control when data is considered stale
✅ Use `gcTime` (formerly `cacheTime`) to control garbage collection
✅ Use `enabled` option to conditionally execute queries
✅ Use `invalidateQueries` after mutations to refetch stale data
✅ Use `optimisticUpdates` via `onMutate` for instant UI feedback
✅ Prefer object syntax for hooks — `useQuery({ queryKey, queryFn })`
⛔ NEVER use v4 `cacheTime` — renamed to `gcTime` in v5
⛔ NEVER fetch in `useEffect` when React Query can handle it
⛔ NEVER forget to handle loading and error states
⛔ NEVER mutate query data directly — use `setQueryData` or invalidate

---

## React Hook Form 7 + Zod 4

<!-- Source: React Hook Form docs, Zod docs -->

✅ Use `useForm` with `zodResolver` for schema validation
✅ Define schemas with Zod — `z.object({ name: z.string().min(1) })`
✅ Use `register` to connect inputs to form state
✅ Use `handleSubmit` for form submission — validation runs automatically
✅ Use `formState.errors` to display validation errors
✅ Use `control` with `Controller` for custom components (Radix UI, etc.)
✅ Use `watch` to subscribe to form field changes
✅ Use `setValue` to programmatically update fields
✅ Use `reset` to clear form or set new default values
✅ Use `mode: 'onBlur'` or `mode: 'onChange'` for validation timing
✅ Use `defaultValues` to initialize form state
⛔ NEVER validate manually when React Hook Form + Zod can handle it
⛔ NEVER forget to connect form fields with `register` or `Controller`
⛔ NEVER mutate form state directly outside React Hook Form APIs
⛔ NEVER use uncontrolled components without `register`

---

## Radix UI + shadcn/ui

<!-- Source: Radix UI docs, shadcn/ui docs -->

✅ Import from `@/components/ui/*` for shadcn components — project convention
✅ Use Radix primitives as building blocks — `@radix-ui/react-*` packages
✅ Customize via Tailwind classes — shadcn components are not prebuilt, they're source
✅ Use `asChild` prop to compose components without wrapper divs
✅ Disable `react-refresh/only-export-components` for `components/ui/**` — generated code pattern
✅ Keep generated components in `src/components/ui/` separate from app components
✅ Use `Dialog`, `Popover`, `DropdownMenu` for overlays — accessible by default
✅ Use `Form` components from shadcn for React Hook Form integration
✅ Expect all Radix components to handle keyboard navigation, focus management, ARIA
⛔ NEVER modify generated shadcn components without understanding changes — they may be regenerated
⛔ NEVER bypass accessibility features built into Radix — keyboard nav, ARIA roles, focus management
⛔ NEVER use both controlled and uncontrolled patterns on same component
⛔ NEVER ignore Radix component prop types — they encode accessibility requirements

---

## date-fns 4

<!-- Source: date-fns v4 docs -->

✅ Import functions individually — tree-shakeable: `import { format, addDays } from 'date-fns'`
✅ Use `format(date, 'yyyy-MM-dd')` for date formatting
✅ Use `parse`, `parseISO` for date parsing
✅ Use `addDays`, `subDays`, `addMonths` for date arithmetic
✅ Use `isAfter`, `isBefore`, `isEqual` for date comparisons
✅ Use `startOfDay`, `endOfDay`, `startOfWeek` for date boundaries
✅ Pass `Date` objects, not strings — parse first if needed
✅ Use `utcToZonedTime`, `zonedTimeToUtc` from `date-fns-tz` for timezone work
⛔ NEVER mutate date objects — date-fns functions are pure and return new dates
⛔ NEVER use `new Date()` for date arithmetic — use date-fns functions
⛔ NEVER assume dates are in local timezone — be explicit about timezone handling
⛔ NEVER format dates manually with string concatenation

---

## Testing Library

<!-- Source: Testing Library docs, Testing Library best practices -->

✅ Query by role first — `getByRole('button', { name: /submit/i })`
✅ Query by label text for form fields — `getByLabelText('Email address')`
✅ Query by placeholder as fallback — `getByPlaceholderText('Enter email')`
✅ Query by text content for static elements — `getByText('Hello world')`
✅ Use `getBy*` for elements that must exist
✅ Use `queryBy*` for elements that may not exist — returns `null` instead of throwing
✅ Use `findBy*` for elements that appear asynchronously — returns promise
✅ Use `userEvent` from `@testing-library/user-event` for interactions — more accurate than `fireEvent`
✅ Use `screen` for all queries — no need to destructure `render` return value
✅ Use `waitFor` for async assertions — `await waitFor(() => expect(...))`
✅ Use `within(element)` to scope queries to a subtree
⛔ NEVER use `getByTestId` as first choice — prefer semantic queries
⛔ NEVER query for implementation details — test what users see
⛔ NEVER use `container.querySelector` — use Testing Library queries
⛔ NEVER use `fireEvent` when `userEvent` can do it — `userEvent.click` is more realistic than `fireEvent.click`

---

## File Organization

<!-- Inferred from project structure -->

✅ Place React components in `src/components/`
✅ Place hooks in `src/hooks/`
✅ Place utility functions in `src/lib/`
✅ Place tests next to the code they test — `ComponentName.test.tsx`
✅ Place UI primitives in `src/components/ui/` — generated shadcn/ui components
✅ Place styles in `src/styles/` or colocate with components
✅ Use `@/` path alias for imports from `src/` — `import { foo } from '@/lib/utils'`
✅ Use PascalCase for component files — `AppHeader.tsx`, `CalendarGrid.tsx`
✅ Use kebab-case or camelCase for utility files — `date-utils.ts`, `ical-parser.ts`
⛔ NEVER create circular dependencies between modules
⛔ NEVER import from `../../../` — use `@/` path alias
⛔ NEVER mix default and named exports in the same file without clear intent

---

## Build & Deployment

<!-- Source: Project's package.json scripts, wrangler.jsonc -->

✅ Run `npm run dev` for local development server — port 5000
✅ Run `npm run build` to build production bundle
✅ Run `npm run test` to run tests once
✅ Run `npm run test:coverage` to run tests with coverage report
✅ Run `npm run lint` to check code quality
✅ Generate version before build — `prebuild` and `predev` scripts handle this
✅ Deployment via `wrangler` to Cloudflare Workers
✅ Static assets served from `dist/` directory in Workers
⛔ NEVER commit `dist/` or build artifacts
⛔ NEVER skip TypeScript checks in CI — `tsc -b --noCheck` is for local dev only
⛔ NEVER deploy without running tests and linting

---

## Performance

✅ Use React.memo() for expensive components that render often with same props
✅ Use `useMemo` to memoize expensive computations
✅ Use `useCallback` to memoize functions passed as props to memoized children
✅ Use `useDeferredValue` for non-urgent updates
✅ Use `useTransition` for state updates that can be deprioritized
✅ Lazy load routes and large components with `React.lazy` and `Suspense`
✅ Optimize images — use modern formats, appropriate sizes
✅ Code split vendor bundles — Vite handles this by default
✅ Minimize bundle size — avoid unnecessary dependencies
⛔ NEVER optimize prematurely — measure first
⛔ NEVER use `useMemo` or `useCallback` for every function/value — adds overhead
⛔ NEVER skip key prop on list items — causes reconciliation issues

---

## Accessibility

✅ Use semantic HTML — `<button>`, `<nav>`, `<main>`, `<article>`
✅ Use Radix UI components for complex widgets — accessibility built-in
✅ Ensure keyboard navigation works — tab order, focus management
✅ Use ARIA labels where needed — `aria-label`, `aria-labelledby`, `aria-describedby`
✅ Test with screen readers — NVDA on Windows, VoiceOver on macOS
✅ Ensure color contrast meets WCAG AA — use contrast checker
✅ Provide text alternatives for images — `alt` attribute
✅ Ensure focus is visible — `:focus-visible` styling
⛔ NEVER use `div` or `span` as button — use `<button>`
⛔ NEVER remove focus outlines without providing alternative focus indication
⛔ NEVER rely on color alone to convey information
⛔ NEVER use `role="button"` on a div when `<button>` works
